nodes.name: Green parakeet
nodes.summary: Green parakeet（Green parakeet，学名 Psittacara holochlorus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于世界鸟类学家联合会、墨西哥、Gallery forest，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 墨西哥
nodes.summary: 墨西哥是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联249种鸟类，包括灰胸穴䳍、棕穴䳍、草黃樹鴨等249种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 世界鸟类学家联合会
nodes.summary: 世界鸟类学家联合会是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括Tasmanian boobook、Green parakeet、Fiery-capped manakin等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Gallery forest
nodes.summary: Gallery forest是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联206种鸟类，包括Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan、黑額鳴冠雉等206种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: As defined by the International Ornithological Committee (IOC), it is native to Mexico and southern Texas in the Rio Grande Valley.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/parrots/ |title=Parrots, cockatoos |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 13.1 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

links.evidence: As defined by the International Ornithological Committee (IOC), it is native to Mexico and southern Texas in the Rio Grande Valley.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/parrots/ |title=Parrots, cockatoos |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 13.1 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Williams III | date=August 16, 2022|publisher=New Mexico Bird Records Committee |access-date=April 6, 2023 }} In its native range, the green parakeet inhabits semi-open landscapes including deciduous woodland, gallery forest, and scrublands

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Williams III | date=August 16, 2022|publisher=New Mexico Bird Records Committee |access-date=April 6, 2023 }} In its native range, the green parakeet inhabits semi-open landscapes including deciduous woodland, gallery forest, and scrublands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats to the Mexican population have been identified. NatureServe considers the species Vulnerable in Texas due to hunting pressure
